title: Nintendo Switch 2 Launch Title Leaks: 8 Games Unveiled by European Publisher
excerpt: A server leak from a major European game publisher has exposed 8 launch titles for the upcoming Nintendo Switch 2, including a new Mario Kart and Metroid Prime.
bodyHtml:
The gaming community has been set ablaze by a massive security breach at a major European game publisher. A leaked production schedule document has surfaced on developer forums, detailing **8 launch title games** specifically formatted for the highly anticipated **Nintendo Switch 2** console.

The Leaked Launch Lineup

The leaked files provide details for both first-party classics and third-party blockbusters optimized for the Switch 2's Nvidia T239 chipset. The highlights of the list include:

  • **Mario Kart 9: Horizon** - Built from the ground up to showcase the console's ray-tracing capabilities.
  • **Metroid Prime 4: Remastered Edition** - Featuring 4K resolution at a stable 60 FPS when docked.
  • **Legend of Zelda: Echoes of the Past** - A brand-new 3D spinoff focusing on dungeon exploration.
  • **Monster Hunter Wilds: Switch Edition** - Featuring DLSS upscaling to match console gameplay parity.
"If this list is real, this is the strongest launch title lineup in Nintendo history. Having Mario Kart and Zelda at day one is absolute gold."
— Gamer forum review

Specs Alignment and Developers Dev Kits

The document confirms that all 8 titles are designed to utilize the Switch 2's high-bandwidth system memory and custom SSD storage. Game loading times are reportedly reduced to under 3 seconds using custom asset streaming blocks. Furthermore, developers are utilizing Nvidia's DLSS reconstruction profiles to dynamically upscale graphics from 1080p handheld resolutions to 4K display formats when docked.

Nintendo's Security Lockdown

Nintendo has already launched an aggressive legal response, issuing copyright takedown requests on all mirrors of the leaked document. However, the details have already spread across social networks, with hardware analysts predicting an official Nintendo Direct announcement is imminent to contain the marketing narrative before launch details are further diluted.
